regimeSub: Warsh signals July rate decision imminent at Sintra; AI/HBM rally defies 4.2% CPI overhang.
themes: Fed Chair Warsh at ECB Sintra vowed to 'disappoint' inflation doves, flagged rate decision within 4 weeks → USD bid (DXY 101.4), gold worst monthly drop since 2008
BOJ hiked to 1% in June and ends tapering FY2027 → USDJPY grinding 162.5 as real rate gap persists despite nominal tightening
SK Hynix overtook Samsung in market cap on HBM dominance → KOSPI +68% Q2 (best since 1998) but KRW crashed through 1550 on foreign rotation
Q1 GDP revised to 2.1% with May CPI at 4.2% YoY → stagflation whispers as retail sales mask weakening volume growth

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: 10Y nominal 4.44% vs 4.2% CPI = barely positive real rate; Warsh hawkish lean puts upside risk on front-end

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: 2s at 4.14%, 10s at 4.44% = +30bp steepening; market pricing term premium return as Fed holds

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Risk-on persists despite hawkish Fed rhetoric; spreads contained as AI capex cycle funds corporate balance sheets

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 16.59 complacent given Warsh July decision uncertainty; vol sellers in control post-Sintra

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: Brent $70.69 collapsed from $120 peak as Hormuz reopens; Iran war premium fully unwound

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $4069 after worst monthly drop since 2008; hawkish Warsh + stronger USD crushing safe haven bid

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1553 breached as foreigners dumped $942M in 'Sell Korea' rout despite KOSPI rally; classic EM FX disconnect

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: Nvidia +85% Q1 rev, TSMC raised capex guidance; HBM demand structural but valuation stretched at cycle peak

headline: New Fed Chair Warsh's hawkish Sintra debut puts July hike in play while AI-driven equity euphoria collides with 4.2% inflation and post-Iran war oil unwind.
